text:
was a member of the Eldian Restorationists.

Appearance

Grice was a well-built young man of average height with short blond hair and light-colored eyes. He was seen wearing white collard buttoned shirt and black trousers. As a sign of his affiliation to the Eldian Restorationists, he had a cross-shaped scar on his right shoulder.

Personality

Grice showed to be open and calm towards Grisha and the rest of the Restorationists in the beginning. However, in the face of danger, it appeared that Grice had a tendency to panic and spoke before he thought. He did not seem to keep a level head when dealing with critical situations.

History

in 824, during a visit to Grisha Yeager's clinic, Grice informed him of his sister Faye's fate and offered to tell Grisha more if he would agree to join the Eldian Restorationists. Grice is present at the secret meetings of the Restorationists. When Grisha deciphers the ancient documents provided to them by the Owl, Grice is impressed by Grisha's ability to interpret them, but Grisha claims to not understand most of it himself.

Eight years later, Grice, Grisha, and the other members were arrested due to Grisha's son telling the Public Security Authorities of their organization and for straying from the Marleyan law. They would go on to be tortured before being sent off to roam on Paradis Island as mindless Titans. Grice was led to the top of the Wall in restraints. Once he found Grisha, Grice felt betrayed, blaming the Restorationists' fate on Grisha and his son. He was kicked off the Wall by Sergeant Major Gross, and left to be fed to the Titans - the remaining members of the Restorationists. While no one witnessed his death, Kruger later informed Grisha that reaching the Walls of Paradis Island without the power of the Titans was an impossibility. Being an ordinary man, Grice would eventually be caught and eaten by the Titans.
